danger_signals.visual_form: Incoming sprite animation, screen position movement toward player
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring evasion or destruction

danger_signals.visual_form: Large enemy sprite, distinct visual design compared to standard enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Stage completion obstacle, high danger state

danger_signals.visual_form: Bullet sprites moving across screen from enemy positions
Health bar reduction or loss of visual integrity on player sprite

safety_signals.visual_form: P items (weapon upgrade), F items (rare), B items (special attack charges) dropping from defeated enemies

safety_signals.meaning: Increased player resilience, world state less immediately threatening

safety_signals.visual_form: Absence of enemy sprites in player vicinity
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary safety window for movement and positioning

safety_signals.signal: Score items from defeated ground enemies
safety_signals.meaning: Progress toward resource goals, strategic advantage accumulation

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Stage 1-3 randomly selectable from four nation stages; US stage avoided by US characters
Dedicated stage in random selection pool
Dedicated stage in random selection pool
Dedicated stage in random selection pool

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Standard enemy density and behavior patterns
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Basic projectile patterns, standard enemy movement speeds

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Increased enemy density on screen, more complex visual arrangements
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Coordinated multi-enemy attack patterns, boss encounter preparation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Single large enemy sprite with distinct visual design, fills significant screen real estate
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Complex attack patterns, multiple phase cycles, telegraphed special attacks

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Replication of stages 1-7 with intensified visual density
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy behavior becomes more aggressive, attack patterns more complex, spawn rates increase

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character's nation stage visually distinct but never appears for that character in random selection
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Stage pool restriction creates narrative distance between pilot and homeland

environmental_storytelling.example: Blaster Keaton pilots Boeing F/A-18 Hornet (US); Hien pilots Mitsubishi FSX (Japan). Visual distinctiveness of aircraft models communicates national identity
Score items dropped by ground enemies appear as nation-specific currency symbols, reinforcing faction identity through resource representation
Characters never fight in their home nation's stage (stages 1-3), suggesting characters deployed to foreign airspace or theaters
Each pilot pair visually distinct through character portrait sprites and naming conventions (Kohful 'The Viking' signals Nordic identity)

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Approach player position, fire projectiles on schedule or proximity trigger
Drop score items (currency) when destroyed, providing resource feedback
Complex multi-phase attack patterns with telegraphed special moves, requires sustained evasion and damage output
Weapon enhancement (P items) increases fire rate/damage visual representation; special attack (B items) shows distinct visual effect
Reduced visual integrity or sprite changes indicate health loss; additional life loss shows character sprite reset to default state

notes: Aero Fighters communicates world-state primarily through combat intensity escalation rather than environmental narrative. The four-nation faction system is visualized through character-aircraft pairing and nation-specific currency, but lacks detailed environmental or atmospheric differentiation in available documentation. The game's political dimension (international military pilot competition) is implicit in character selection rather than explicitly manifested through stage design or enemy faction identification. Danger/safety signals rely entirely on standard shmup visual language: projectiles, sprite proximity, health indicators, and power-up drops. No faction-specific enemy visual variants are documented.
